title: Building a Crypto Store on GitHub Pages: Everything You Need to Get Started — No PayPal Required
author: Unchained Commerce
excerpt: From zero to live crypto store in one afternoon. GitHub Pages hosting, Core Shop Engine, Web3 Wallet Connect, NOWPayments integration.

content.text: Are you a digital creator in Nigeria, Ghana, Kenya, or Pakistan struggling to sell your ebooks, courses, or software due to PayPal's country restrictions? You're not alone. Thousands of creators like you face this same challenge. But what if you could set up a crypto store on GitHub Pages without needing PayPal? Let's get started on this journey together, and I'll show you exactly how to do it, step-by-step, without any corporate jargon or fluff.
Why This Method Works When Everything Else Fails

This method works because it utilizes GitHub Pages as a static site host, allowing you to deploy your store without requiring a traditional web server or database. Additionally, by integrating the Core Shop Engine and Web3 Wallet Connect, you can create a seamless payment and delivery pipeline that's both secure and scalable. The beauty of this solution lies in its simplicity and flexibility, making it ideal for creators who need a quick and reliable way to monetize their digital products.
What You Need Before You Start

Before you begin, make sure you have a GitHub account, a digital product to sell, and a payment processor like NOWPayments integrated into your store. You'll also need to set up your Core Shop Engine and Web3 Wallet Connect instances, as well as configure your Admin Dashboard to manage orders and analytics. Don't worry if this sounds overwhelming – we'll break it down into manageable steps, and I'll guide you through the process.
Setting Up Your Payment and Delivery Pipeline

To set up your payment pipeline, you'll need to integrate NOWPayments into your Core Shop Engine instance. This will enable you to accept crypto payments from customers without requiring a traditional payment processor like PayPal. Next, configure your Web3 Wallet Connect instance to allow customers to pay with their preferred cryptocurrencies. Finally, set up your Admin Dashboard to track orders and handle customer support.
You can set up a crypto store on GitHub Pages without needing PayPal, giving you the freedom to monetize your digital products without restrictions.
Going Live and Making Your First Sale

Once you've set up your payment and delivery pipeline, it's time to go live and make your first sale. Test your store thoroughly to ensure everything is working smoothly, then launch it to the public. Promote your store on social media and through email marketing to attract customers. With the Full Stack Bundle, you'll have access to priority updates, Discord support, and a deployment guide to help you succeed.
Scaling Once the System Is Running

As your store grows and you start receiving more sales, you'll want to scale your infrastructure to handle the increased traffic. This means upgrading your Core Shop Engine instance, adding more storage to your GitHub Pages repository, and possibly even hiring a developer to help you manage your store's growth. With the Full Stack Bundle, you'll have access to priority updates and Discord support to help you navigate this process.
How do I build a digital store that accepts crypto payments?

The correct setup sequence matters because each component depends on the previous one being configured correctly. Start with NOWPayments: create your account, add your wallet addresses for each coin you want to accept, and generate your API key. Then deploy the Core Shop Engine: upload your product files, configure your NOWPayments API credentials, set your product prices in USD, and deploy to your hosting environment. Then configure Web3 Wallet Connect: connect it to your Core Shop Engine endpoint, set up your product display, and test the checkout flow in a browser. Finally, complete a real mainnet test transaction with a small amount of actual crypto. Confirm the webhook fires, the engine processes it, the delivery email arrives, and the download link works. Only after that test passes should you start driving traffic to your store.
The Core Shop Engine is the backbone of your store, managing payments, deliveries, and analytics in one seamless pipeline.

If you're looking for more information on setting up a crypto store on GitHub Pages, be sure to check out our tutorial on integrating NOWPayments with the Core Shop Engine. Alternatively, if you're interested in taking your store to the next level, consider investing in the Full Stack Bundle, which includes everything you need to succeed – Core Engine, Wallet Connect, Dashboard, plus priority updates, Discord support, and a deployment guide – all for just $79.99.
Your Action Checklist

To successfully set up your crypto store on GitHub Pages, follow these steps: Create a GitHub repository for your store and configure your Core Shop Engine instance. Integrate NOWPayments into your Core Shop Engine instance to accept crypto payments. Set up your Web3 Wallet Connect instance to allow customers to pay with their preferred cryptocurrencies. Configure your Admin Dashboard to track orders and handle customer support. Test your store thoroughly before going live. Promote your store on social media and through email marketing to attract customers. Monitor your store's analytics and adjust your strategy as needed. Finally, consider investing in the Full Stack Bundle to ensure you have access to priority updates and Discord support.
